input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI targeting Android + Windows on Windows 11:

---

## 1. Install .NET SDK (latest with MAUI support)

```powershell
# Discover latest .NET SDK versions
winget search Microsoft.DotNet.SDK

# Install (adjust version as needed)
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.8 --silent

# Verify
dotnet --list-sdks
```

## 2. Install the MAUI Workload

```powershell
# List available workloads to find current MAUI name
dotnet workload search maui

# Install MAUI (includes Android, Windows targets)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

## 3. Install Java JDK (required for Android)

```powershell
# Discover available JDK versions
winget search Microsoft.OpenJDK

# Install (MAUI requires JDK 11+)
winget install Microsoft.OpenJDK.17 --silent

# Verify
java --version
```

## 4. Install Android SDK & Accept Licenses

```powershell
# The MAUI workload installs Android SDK components, but you can check/manage them:
# Find where Android SDK was provisioned
$androidSdkPath = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Android\sdk"

# If you need the standalone command-line tools:
winget install Google.AndroidCommandLineTools --silent

# Discover installed Android platforms & components
& "$androidSdkPath\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list

# Install specific platform (discover latest dynamically)
& "$androidSdkPath\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
  "platforms;android-34" "build-tools;34.0.0" "platform-tools"

# Accept all licenses
& "$androidSdkPath\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --licenses
```

## 5. Set Environment Variables

```powershell
# Set ANDROID_HOME (persists across sessions)
[Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME", "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Microsoft\Android\sdk", "User")

# Set JAVA_HOME
$javaPath = (Get-Command java).Source | Split-Path | Split-Path
[Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("JAVA_HOME", $javaPath, "User")
```

## 6. Enable Windows App SDK / WinUI (for Windows target)

```powershell
# Usually included with MAUI workload; verify:
dotnet workload list | Select-String "maui-windows"

# If missing:
dotnet workload install maui-windows
```

## 7. Enable Developer Mode (required for sideloading Windows apps)

```powershell
# Enable Developer Mode via registry
reg add "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
  /t REG_DWORD /f /v "AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense" /d 1
```

## 8. Create & Build a Test Project

```powershell
# List MAUI templates
dotnet new list maui

# Create a new project
dotnet new maui -n MauiTestApp
cd MauiTestApp

# Build for Windows
dotnet build -f net8.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net8.0-android
```

## 9. Run on Android Emulator

```powershell
# List available system images
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list | Select-String "system-images"

# Install an emulator image
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" "system-images;android-34;google_apis;x86_64"

# Create AVD
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat" create avd `
  -n "Pixel_API34" -k "system-images;android-34;google_apis;x86_64" --force

# Launch emulator (async)
Start-Process "$env:ANDROID_HOME\emulator\emulator.exe" -ArgumentList "-avd Pixel_API34"

# Deploy
dotnet run -f net8.0-android
```

## 10. (Optional) Install Visual Studio for GUI Tooling

```powershell
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community --silent `
  --override "--add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.ManagedDesktop --passive"
```

---

**Key tips:**
- Replace `net8.0` with `net9.0` if using .NET 9 — discover via `dotnet --list-sdks`.
- Run `dotnet workload update` periodically to stay current.
- If builds fail with SDK errors, run `dotnet workload repair`.
